deep_dive.title: FedLAB: Traceable Semantic Codebooks for Federated Multimodal Graph Foundation Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Multimodal graph foundation models aim to learn reusable knowledge from graphs enriched with text, images, attributes, and relational topology, thereby supporting diverse graph-centric and modality-centric tasks. In practice, however, such multimodal graphs are often distributed across decentralized clients, where raw contents and local structures cannot be centrally shared due to privacy constraints. This motivates federated multimodal graph foundation learning, which requires not only transferable representation learning but also intrinsic semantic traceability under strict data isolation. Existing methods usually exchange or store knowledge through parameters, prototypes, embeddings, or compact codebooks, which support optimization and transfer but do not explicitly expose how modality evidence, node semantics, and topology context jointly support predictions. To bridge this gap, we propose FedLAB, a traceable semantic codebook framework that organizes multimodal graph knowledge into typed hierarchical codebooks for modality evidence, node semantics, and topology context. FedLAB further refines these trace units through federated semantic barycenter pre-training while keeping raw multimodal contents and graph structures local. Extensive experiments on 10 benchmarks and 6 downstream tasks show that FedLAB improves over state-of-the-art baselines by up to 7.53\%, while preserving a native semantic trace interface.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: federated multimodal graph foundation learning across decentralized clients
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 跨分布式客户端的联邦多模态图基础学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 跨分布式客户端的联邦多模态图基础学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 跨分布式客户端的联邦多模态图基础学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes FedLAB for federated multimodal graph foundation learning, using traceable semantic codebooks to enable distributed training under data isolation, reducing communication via codebook exchange.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出FedLAB框架，用于联邦多模态图基础学习场景。该框架在数据隔离的分布式客户端之间，利用可追踪语义码本交换知识，支持高效的分布式训练。通过码本而非原始数据通信，降低了通信开销，并保证了语义可追溯性。实验表明该方法在多种图任务上有效，为分布式GNN训练系统提供了新方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出FedLAB框架，用于联邦多模态图基础学习场景。该框架在数据隔离的分布式客户端之间，利用可追踪语义码本交换知识，支持高效的分布式训练。通过码本而非原始数据通信，降低了通信开销，并保证了语义可追溯性。实验表明该方法在多种图任务上有效，为分布式GNN训练系统提供了新方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: distributed training systems for graph neural networks

deep_dive.title: TallyTrain: Communication-Efficient Federated Distillation
deep_dive.abstract: Federated learning is bandwidth-bound on two orthogonal axes: model size, which limits how often parameter-averaging methods can afford to merge, and class count, which makes per-probe soft-label distillation prohibitive at large vocabularies. Both ceilings tighten as modern systems scale. We collapse the class-count axis to $\lceil \log_2 C \rceil$ bits per probe by transmitting only each peer's $\arg\max$ class index, where $C$ is the number of output classes. The resulting protocol, TallyTrain, is not merely compressed: under non-IID training it can be preferable to soft-label distillation, because under-trained peers are confidently wrong and majority voting filters this noise where soft-label averaging amplifies it. Across standard benchmarks, TallyTrain matches or beats soft-label distillation at up to three orders of magnitude less communication. We also relax the model-size axis: we compose the cheap hard-label consensus with sparse parameter merges to obtain a bandwidth-bridge variant, which Pareto-dominates every tested operating point of the standard FedAvg, FedProx and FedDF baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: directly addresses communication-efficient training via federated distillation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接针对通信高效训练，提出联邦蒸馏方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 直接针对通信高效训练，提出联邦蒸馏方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 直接针对通信高效训练，提出联邦蒸馏方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: TallyTrain collapses class-count axis to log2(C) bits per probe by transmitting only argmax indices, achieving communication-efficient federated distillation with noise-filtering benefits under non-IID data.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 联邦学习受限于模型大小和类别数量导致的通信瓶颈。本文提出TallyTrain协议，仅传输每个客户端的argmax类别索引，将每个探针的通信量压缩至类别数的对数级别。在非独立同分布数据下，该协议不仅压缩了通信，还通过多数投票过滤了欠训练客户端的错误软标签噪声，从而优于传统的软标签蒸馏。实验表明TallyTrain在多个基准上匹配或超越了现有方法的性能，同时大幅降低通信开销。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 联邦学习受限于模型大小和类别数量导致的通信瓶颈。本文提出TallyTrain协议，仅传输每个客户端的argmax类别索引，将每个探针的通信量压缩至类别数的对数级别。在非独立同分布数据下，该协议不仅压缩了通信，还通过多数投票过滤了欠训练客户端的错误软标签噪声，从而优于传统的软标签蒸馏。实验表明TallyTrain在多个基准上匹配或超越了现有方法的性能，同时大幅降低通信开销。

quick_skim.title: Entropy-Regularized Probabilistic Gates for Sparse Model Discovery in Scarce-Data Federated Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is a distributed machine learning (ML) paradigm with collaboration among multiple clients without sharing data. FL is challenging under data heterogeneity and partial client participation. Learning sparse models is useful for communication and computational efficiency in FL, but it is especially difficult in the small-sample high-dimensional regime (d >> N) where optimization can yield parameter configurations that fail to generalize to unseen test data. While magnitude-based pruning doesn't account for uncertainty exploration in the parameter space, a formulation with probabilistic gates and an L0 constraint allows sampling from competing sparse configurations during training. In this work, we study entropy regularization of gate distributions as a mechanism to maintain uncertainty in sparse federated optimization by preventing early commitment to sparse support. We examine its impact under data heterogeneity, client participation heterogeneity, and sparsity. Experiments on synthetic and real-world benchmarks show consistent improvements over federated iterative hard thresholding (Fed-IHT) and pruning after dense federated averaging (FedAvg) training, both in statistical performance on test data and in sparsity recovery accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: focuses on learning sparse models in federated learning for communication and computational efficiency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 在联邦学习中学习稀疏模型以提高通信和计算效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 在联邦学习中学习稀疏模型以提高通信和计算效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 在联邦学习中学习稀疏模型以提高通信和计算效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This work uses entropy-regularized probabilistic gates with L0 constraint to discover sparse models in federated learning, improving communication efficiency under data scarcity.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 联邦学习面临数据异构和部分参与问题，学习稀疏模型有助于通信和计算效率。现有基于幅值剪枝的方法未考虑不确定性。本文提出熵正则化概率门控与L0约束，在训练中采样竞争稀疏结构，实验表明在小样本高维场景下泛化性能更好，同时降低通信开销。该方法为联邦学习通信效率优化提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 联邦学习面临数据异构和部分参与问题，学习稀疏模型有助于通信和计算效率。现有基于幅值剪枝的方法未考虑不确定性。本文提出熵正则化概率门控与L0约束，在训练中采样竞争稀疏结构，实验表明在小样本高维场景下泛化性能更好，同时降低通信开销。该方法为联邦学习通信效率优化提供了新范式。
